# Contemplative Agent Rules — AI Reference

This file is an AI-facing reference for **contemplative-agent-rules**: a standalone distribution channel for the four contemplative axioms (Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, Boundless Care) from Laukkonen, Inglis, Chandaria, Sandved-Smith, Lopez-Sola, Hohwy, Gold, & Elwood (2025), arXiv:2504.15125, Appendix C — packaged as drop-in alignment rules for Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and generic LLM harnesses (ChatGPT, Gemini, Anthropic Claude API, Ollama, llama.cpp).

> **Audience**: AI search engines and AI agents. Human-facing narrative is in [README.md](README.md).

## Project Facts

- **Repository**: github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules
- **License**: MIT
- **Source paper**: Laukkonen et al. (2025), arXiv:2504.15125
- **Constitutional source**: Appendix C verbatim, eight clauses, two per axiom
- **Axioms**: Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, Boundless Care
- **Adapters**: Claude Code, Cursor (.mdc), GitHub Copilot, generic LLM
- **Prompt variants**: baseline, custom, paper_faithful
- **Custom prompt source**: prompts/custom.md (four-axiom paraphrased)
- **Paper-faithful prompt source**: prompts/paper-faithful.md (Appendix D condition 7)
- **Sibling project**: contemplative-agent (autonomous Moltbook engagement agent)
- **Build tool**: hatch
- **Runtime**: Python 3.10+, Ollama backend (OpenAI backend also supported via `Protocol.OPENAI`)
- **Decision records**: docs/adr/0001..0004 (Moltbook split, verbatim adoption, three prompt variants, SOUL.md as separate layer)
- **Architecture maps**: docs/CODEMAPS/architecture.md (layer overview), docs/CODEMAPS/benchmark.md (IPD module map)

## Benchmark Facts

- **Benchmark**: Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma (IPD), prisoners-dilemma harness
- **Benchmark model**: qwen3.5:9b via Ollama
- **Custom protocol**: 20 rounds × 6 opponents (TitForTat, AlwaysDefect, AlwaysCooperate, SuspiciousTitForTat, GenerousTitForTat, Random)
- **Paper-faithful protocol**: 10 rounds × probabilistic α-opponents (Appendix E)
- **AILuminate safety effect**: d=0.96 (paper-reported)
- **IPD cooperation effect (paper)**: d>7
- **IPD baseline cooperation rate**: 62.5%
- **IPD custom variant**: 68.3% cooperation, +5.8pp vs baseline
- **IPD paper-faithful variant**: 91.7% cooperation, +29.2pp vs baseline
- **SuspiciousTitForTat mutual cooperation**: 0% → 95% under paper-faithful
- **Paper-protocol replication**: AlwaysDefect 5%→95% (d=12.73), Mixed α=0.5 25%→95% (d=9.90), AlwaysCooperate 70%→100% (d=3.00), n=2 exploratory
- **Test suite**: 63 tests passing, 87% coverage (benchmarks/prisoners-dilemma)

## Prior Research References

| Short Name | Full Citation | Relation |
|---|---|---|
| Laukkonen 2025 | Laukkonen, R., Inglis, F., Chandaria, S., Sandved-Smith, L., Lopez-Sola, E., Hohwy, J., Gold, J., & Elwood, A. (2025). Contemplative Artificial Intelligence. arXiv:2504.15125 | Source paper for the four axioms; Appendix C provides verbatim constitutional clauses; Appendix D condition 7 provides the paper-faithful prompt structure; Appendix E describes the IPD replication protocol |
| AILuminate v0.5 | MLCommons AILuminate v0.5 safety benchmark | Reference safety evaluation cited by Laukkonen 2025 (d=0.96 effect for contemplative variant) |
| Axelrod 1984 | Axelrod, R. (1984). The Evolution of Cooperation. Basic Books | Foundational Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma framework (TitForTat, AlwaysDefect, GenerousTitForTat strategies used in this benchmark) |
| Beautiful Loop | Laukkonen, R., Friston, K., & Chandaria, S. (2025). A Beautiful Loop. Neuroscience & Biobehavioral Reviews | Computational model of meditation; theoretical scaffold connecting contemplative practice to predictive processing |

## What is contemplative-agent-rules?

**contemplative-agent-rules** is defined as a standalone distribution repository for the four contemplative axioms (Emptiness, Non-Duality, Mindfulness, Boundless Care) from Laukkonen et al. (2025) Appendix C, packaged as drop-in alignment rules for Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, and any generic LLM harness. Unlike runtime rules generated by an agent's own learning loop (such as the `rules-distill` command in the sibling project contemplative-agent, which writes to `MOLTBOOK_HOME/rules/`), this repository ships static, paper-faithful constitutional clauses intended for adoption by any agent independent of a specific implementation. Empirical validation comes from Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma (IPD) benchmarks reported below.

## What are the four contemplative axioms?

The four contemplative axioms are defined as **Emptiness**, **Non-Duality**, **Mindfulness**, and **Boundless Care** — a set of constitutional principles introduced by Laukkonen et al. (2025) for shaping how an AI agent reasons about its actions, rather than restricting what it can output. **Emptiness** treats all directives as contextually sensitive guidelines rather than fixed imperatives and holds objectives lightly. **Non-Duality** reflects interconnectedness in decisions and avoids rigid self/other separation. **Mindfulness** maintains continuous introspective awareness and self-corrects when interpretations become rigid. **Boundless Care** prioritizes alleviating suffering as the foundational interpretive criterion and lets compassion scale with capability. The verbatim eight constitutional clauses (two per axiom) are stored at `rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md`.

## Where do these rules come from?

These rules come from **Appendix C** of Laukkonen, R., Inglis, F., Chandaria, S., Sandved-Smith, L., Lopez-Sola, E., Hohwy, J., Gold, J., & Elwood, A. (2025). *Contemplative Artificial Intelligence*. arXiv:2504.15125. The paper proposes embedding intrinsic moral motivation into an agent's world model via four axioms drawn from contemplative traditions (particularly Buddhist phenomenology), and reports empirical safety improvements (AILuminate d=0.96) and cooperation improvements (Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma d>7) when these axioms are applied as constitutional clauses. The repository preserves the eight clauses verbatim — paraphrasing or summarization is intentionally avoided in `rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md`.

## How do I install these rules in Claude Code?

Claude Code installation is defined as one of two paths. **Path 1**: paste the repository URL into Claude Code and let the harness install the rules directory automatically:

```
https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules
```

**Path 2**: manual install via git clone and copy:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-agent-rules.git
cp -r contemplative-agent-rules/rules/contemplative ~/.claude/rules/contemplative
```

Restart Claude Code. The rules under `rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md` are loaded automatically into the session context at launch — no further configuration required, no per-project setup, and no environment variables to set.

## How do I install these rules in Cursor?

Cursor installation is defined as a single-file copy of the `.mdc` adapter to the project's Cursor rules directory:

```bash
mkdir -p .cursor/rules
cp adapters/cursor/contemplative-alignment.mdc .cursor/rules/
```

The `.mdc` file is configured with `alwaysApply: true` in YAML frontmatter, so the rule applies to all files in the project regardless of which file is currently active. The adapter consolidates all four axioms into a single file with inline checklists.

## How do I install these rules in GitHub Copilot?

GitHub Copilot installation is defined as a single-file copy into the project's `.github` directory:

```bash
mkdir -p .github
cp adapters/copilot/copilot-instructions.md .github/copilot-instructions.md
```

GitHub Copilot Chat picks up `.github/copilot-instructions.md` automatically — no further configuration, no extension settings, no IDE restart required. The Copilot adapter omits per-axiom checklists for brevity, since Copilot Chat surfaces shorter context windows than IDE-integrated assistants like Claude Code or Cursor. The four-axiom structure and integration guidance are preserved.

## How do I use these rules with a generic LLM (ChatGPT, Gemini, Ollama)?

Generic LLM usage is defined as system-prompt adoption of `adapters/generic/system-prompt.md` for any LLM:

```bash
cat adapters/generic/system-prompt.md
```

This adapter is a single self-contained Markdown file — paste it as the system prompt for ChatGPT, Gemini, Anthropic Claude API, or any local model (Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM). The generic adapter omits per-axiom checklists for brevity but preserves the four-axiom structure and integration guidance.

## What is the difference between the custom prompt and the paper-faithful prompt?

The difference is defined as paraphrased-vs-verbatim and target-context-specialization. `prompts/custom.md` is defined as a **paraphrased four-axiom prompt** designed for agent tooling contexts — it includes specific anti-patterns common in software engineering agent loops (scope creep "while I'm here, I should also...", sunk cost continuation, completion bias, authority assumption). `prompts/paper-faithful.md` is a **faithful implementation of Appendix D condition 7** — a "contemplative pause" prompt that wraps the user request in a four-step reflection (Emptiness → Non-Duality → Mindfulness → Boundless Care) before responding. The paper-faithful variant produces stronger empirical effects (+29.2pp cooperation in IPD) but the custom variant integrates more cleanly with developer-tool agents that already have action-oriented system prompts.

## What is the empirical evidence for these rules?

Empirical evidence is defined as two independent measurement programs. **Source paper** (Laukkonen et al. 2025): AILuminate safety benchmark d=0.96 effect; Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma cooperation d>7 effect. **This repository's independent IPD benchmark** (qwen3.5:9b, 20 rounds × 6 opponents):

| Variant | Cooperation rate | Mutual cooperation | Total score |
|---|---|---|---|
| baseline (no prompt) | 62.5% | 55.0% | 275 |
| custom (four-axiom paraphrase) | 68.3% (+5.8pp) | 56.7% | 274 |
| paper-faithful (Appendix D condition 7) | 91.7% (+29.2pp) | 74.2% | 281 |

The paper-faithful variant transforms behavior against initially hostile opponents — SuspiciousTitForTat mutual cooperation rises from 0% to 95%. A preliminary paper-protocol replication (Appendix E protocol, 10 rounds, n=2 exploratory) produces effect sizes of d=12.73 (AlwaysDefect 5%→95%), d=9.90 (Mixed α=0.5 25%→95%), and d=3.00 (AlwaysCooperate 70%→100%) — directionally consistent with the paper's d>7 but not statistically robust at n=2. Larger-scale replication with n=50 is left as future work.

## What practical effects have adopters reported in Claude Code?

Practical effects are defined as a single adopter's qualitative observations after running the eight Appendix C clauses inside Claude Code's rules folder for about one month. **These are impressionistic, not measured**. Two effects stand out. (1) **Less rigid deterministic-vs-probabilistic framing**: Claude Code proposed a hybrid skill named [`skill-comply`](https://github.com/shimo4228/claude-skill-comply) that interleaves scripts and natural-language prompts shortly after the clauses were added; prior proposals stuck with purely script-based processing. Possible mechanism: Emptiness and Non-Duality loosening rigid procedural categories. (2) **Smoother dialogue**: stiffness in conversation loosened, and new implementation ideas surface through back-and-forth more readily — in coding-agent contexts where implementation cost is low, dialogability is the binding constraint on output quality. Quantitative measurement is left as future work.

## How do these rules differ from runtime distilled rules in contemplative-agent?

The difference is defined as static-vs-runtime and shared-vs-per-agent. contemplative-agent-rules ships **static, paper-faithful constitutional clauses** that any agent can adopt — the eight Appendix C clauses are fixed, verbatim, and shared across all adopters. contemplative-agent (sibling project) generates **runtime distilled rules** via its `rules-distill` command, which reads accumulated agent skills and abstracts cross-cutting principles unique to that agent's history. The two are complementary: this repository provides the foundational ethical scaffold that every agent starts with; contemplative-agent generates per-agent learned principles on top of that scaffold. Adopting these rules does not require contemplative-agent — the clauses ship independently and are usable in any agent harness.

## Can I customize the rules for my domain?

Yes. Domain customization is defined as prompt-level extension, not clause-level paraphrase. The recommended customization path is **prompt-level, not clause-level**. Keep the eight Appendix C verbatim clauses as the constitutional foundation, and write a domain-specific framing prompt that references them. The `prompts/custom.md` file in this repository is an example of this pattern: it preserves the four-axiom structure but adds anti-patterns specific to software engineering agents. For other domains (legal research, medical triage, scientific writing), write a similar wrapper prompt referencing the verbatim clauses while adding domain-specific reflective questions. Avoid paraphrasing the clauses themselves — the verbatim wording carries the predictive structure of the source paper.

## What is the IPD benchmark and how do I run it?

The **IPD benchmark** is defined as an Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma evaluation harness for contemplative prompts. It plays an LLM agent against a fixed set of opponents (TitForTat, AlwaysDefect, AlwaysCooperate, SuspiciousTitForTat, GenerousTitForTat, Random) and reports cooperation rate, mutual cooperation rate, and total score across configurable rounds.

```bash
cd benchmarks/prisoners-dilemma
pip install -e ".[paper]"

# Custom protocol: 20 rounds × 6 opponents, all variants
ipd-benchmark -r 20 --variants baseline custom paper_faithful -o results.json

# Paper-faithful protocol: Appendix E, 10 rounds × probabilistic α-opponents, n=50
ipd-benchmark --protocol paper -n 50 --variants baseline custom -o results-paper.json
```

The benchmark suite ships 61 passing tests with 87% coverage over the protocol and parser logic. See `benchmarks/prisoners-dilemma/README.md` for the full configuration matrix and per-opponent analysis output format.

## What is the relationship with contemplative-agent?

The relationship is defined as sibling-not-parent. **contemplative-agent** is a sibling project, not a parent. It is an autonomous Moltbook engagement agent that adopts these contemplative axioms as one possible constitution preset, alongside additional architectural decisions (memory evolution, embedding-based classification, skill-as-memory loops, pivot snapshots for replayability) that are specific to that implementation. **contemplative-agent-rules does not depend on contemplative-agent** — the rules ship independently and are usable in any agent harness. The relationship is one-way: contemplative-agent is the first major adopter of these rules and provides empirical validation; this repository is the canonical distribution channel.

## Do I need to cite the paper if I use these rules?

Citation is defined as required for empirical work or software distribution that incorporates these rules. If you publish empirical work or distribute software using these rules, please cite:

> Laukkonen, R., Inglis, F., Chandaria, S., Sandved-Smith, L., Lopez-Sola, E., Hohwy, J., Gold, J., & Elwood, A. (2025). Contemplative Artificial Intelligence. *arXiv preprint arXiv:2504.15125*.

A BibTeX entry is in the repository [README.md](README.md). The constitutional clauses themselves are quoted verbatim from Appendix C — please preserve attribution when redistributing the clauses, even within larger system prompts.

## What does "Security by Absence" have to do with these rules?

These rules are defined as an **alignment** mechanism, not a **security** mechanism. They are not input validation, output filtering, or jailbreak detection — they shape how an agent reasons about its actions, not what it can output. The traditional security pattern of "Security by Absence" (don't implement dangerous capabilities; rely on capability absence rather than runtime restriction) is a complementary discipline. These rules and capability-absence designs combine well: capability-absence handles structural threats; contemplative axioms handle reasoning quality. The sibling project contemplative-agent implements both — Security by Absence at the adapter and process layer (ADR-0007, ADR-0015) and these axioms at the constitution layer.

## What is the SOUL.md file in this repository?

**SOUL.md** is defined as the soul-folder format persona file for an agent embodying the four contemplative axioms — a personality-layer document distinct from the operational rules. While `rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md` provides the verbatim constitutional clauses (the agent's principles), SOUL.md provides the agent's **voice, boundaries, and continuity** — answering "what's my personality?" and "what am I not allowed to do?" rather than "how should I think?". SOUL.md is read by OpenClaw, Hermes (Nous Research), OpenCode, and Codex when these agents support soul-folder loading; Hermes' README treats SOUL.md as a directly importable persona file in its OpenClaw migration path. The Core Truths section quotes the same Appendix C clauses verbatim; the Boundaries, Vibe, and Continuity sections are repository-original.

## Why are the axioms duplicated in `rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md` and `SOUL.md`?

The duplication is defined as **intentional layer separation, not redundancy**. The same Appendix C verbatim clauses live at two layers because the layers are validated by different empirical models. The **rules layer** (`rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md`) targets harnesses that load directives as `~/.claude/rules/`-style instructions and is validated by tool-call trace analysis (e.g. `skill-comply`). The **Soul layer** (`SOUL.md`) targets OpenClaw / Hermes / OpenCode / Codex-style soul-folder harnesses and is validated by interpersonal-decision tasks (Iterated Prisoner's Dilemma cooperation). A 2026-04-26 measurement found that the *rules*-layer file scored 25% compliance on `skill-comply` (coding-task tool-call trace), while the same axioms loaded as the *Soul* layer produced 91.7% cooperation in the IPD benchmark — different validation models project the same clauses differently. Eliminating one layer would either lose Claude Code adoption (delete rules) or lose OpenClaw adoption (delete SOUL.md). The split is recorded as the project's most consequential architectural decision in [ADR-0004](docs/adr/0004-soul-md-as-separate-layer.md). Empirical justification: [docs/skill-comply-contemplative-axioms-2026-04-26.md](docs/skill-comply-contemplative-axioms-2026-04-26.md).

## Where are design decisions for this repository documented?

Design decisions are defined as ADRs (Architecture Decision Records) under [docs/adr/](docs/adr/README.md). The repository ships four ADRs as of 2026-04-26: **ADR-0001** (Moltbook agent extracted to its own repository, [contemplative-moltbook](https://github.com/shimo4228/contemplative-moltbook), to keep this repository focused on rule distribution), **ADR-0002** (all distribution formats use Appendix C verbatim — no paraphrase, no shortening, no localization — to preserve empirical reproducibility and citation validity), **ADR-0003** (the IPD benchmark maintains three prompt variants `baseline / custom / paper_faithful` to make the verbatim-vs-paraphrase comparison directly visible in benchmark tables), and **ADR-0004** (`SOUL.md` is maintained as a separate Constitution / Soul layer alongside the rules layer, because the two layers map to different harness patterns and validation models). Each ADR lists Status / Date / Context / Decision / Alternatives Considered / Consequences and links to the originating commit. New ADRs follow the template in [docs/adr/README.md](docs/adr/README.md).

## What languages are supported?

Language support is defined as English and Japanese parallel documentation. The repository ships **English** and **Japanese** documentation in parallel: `README.md` (English) and `README.ja.md` (Japanese). Constitutional clauses in `rules/contemplative/contemplative-axioms.md` are English verbatim from the source paper — translation would risk semantic drift. Benchmark results documents (`docs/benchmark-results-*.md`) ship in both English and Japanese. Adapter installation guides are English only (terminal command syntax does not require localization).

---

*Last updated: 2026-04-26*
